etymology.description: The word 素食(素食) (sùshí, "vegetarian food") is based on the combination of 素(素) (sù, "plain; simple") and 食(食) (shí, "food").
etymology.interpretation: 素(素) (sù, "plain; simple") refers to something lacking embellishment or basics, which conveys the idea of foods that are natural and unprocessed. This sense of simplicity extends to plant-based ingredients, often seen as original and wholesome. 食(食) (shí, "food") naturally refers to what is eaten. Thus, the term 素食 refers to foods that are plain in the sense of being plant-based, adhering to a vegetarian diet, without the inclusion of meat or extravagance.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢吃素食(她喜歡吃素食)。
examples.english: She likes to eat vegetarian food.

examples.chinese: 这家餐厅提供很多素食选项(這家餐廳提供很多素食選項)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā cāntīng tígōng hěn duō sùshí xuǎnxiàng.
examples.english: This restaurant offers many vegetarian options.

examples.chinese: 他决定在宴会中只吃素食(他決定在宴會中只吃素食)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juédìng zài yànhuì zhōng zhǐ chī sùshí.
examples.english: He decided to eat only vegetarian food at the banquet.
